positive.name: basic_infinite_loop
positive.code: fn main() { loop { println!("hello"); } }

positive.name: loop_without_break
positive.code: fn main() { let mut i = 0; loop { i += 1; } }

negative.name: loop_with_break
negative.code: fn main() { let mut i = 0; loop { i += 1; if i > 10 { break; } } }

negative.name: while_loop
negative.code: fn main() { let mut i = 0; while i < 10 { i += 1; } }

negative.name: for_loop
negative.code: fn main() { for i in 0..10 { println!("{}", i); } }

edge_cases.name: nested_loops
edge_cases.code: fn main() { loop { loop { break; } break; } }

false_positives.name: tokio_main_loop
false_positives.code: #[tokio::main] async fn main() { loop { tokio::select! { _ = shutdown => break; } } }

positive.name: literal_division_by_zero
positive.code: fn div(a: i32) -> i32 { a / 0 }

positive.name: modulo_by_zero
positive.code: fn mod_zero(a: i32) -> i32 { a % 0 }

negative.name: checked_division
negative.code: fn div(a: i32, b: i32) -> Option<i32> { b.checked_div(a) }

negative.name: nonzero_guard
negative.code: fn div(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 { if b != 0 { a / b } else { 0 } }

edge_cases.name: variable_denominator
edge_cases.code: fn div(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32 { a / b }
edge_cases.reason: Cannot determine at static analysis if b can be zero

false_positives.name: constant_nonzero
false_positives.code: const DIVISOR: i32 = 5; fn div(a: i32) -> i32 { a / DIVISOR }
false_positives.reason: Compile-time constant known to be non-zero

positive.name: mem_uninitialized_call
positive.code: use std::mem; fn foo() { let x: i32 = mem::uninitialized(); }

negative.name: maybe_uninit_zeroed
negative.code: use std::mem::MaybeUninit; fn foo() { let x: MaybeUninit<i32> = MaybeUninit::zeroed(); }

negative.name: proper_maybe_uninit
negative.code: use std::mem::MaybeUninit; fn foo() { let x = MaybeUninit::<i32>::zeroed(); let val = unsafe { x.assume_init() }; }

edge_cases.name: nested_maybe_uninit
edge_cases.code: use std::mem::MaybeUninit; struct Foo { x: i32 } fn foo() { let f: MaybeUninit<Foo> = MaybeUninit::zeroed(); }

positive.name: direct_unwrap_none
positive.code: fn main() { let opt: Option<i32> = None; let val = opt.unwrap(); }

negative.name: if_let_guard
negative.code: fn main() { let opt: Option<i32> = Some(42); if let Some(val) = opt { println!("{}", val); } }

negative.name: match_guard
negative.code: fn main() { let opt: Option<i32> = Some(42); match opt { Some(val) => println!("{}", val), None => println!("none"), } }

edge_cases.name: expect_with_message
edge_cases.code: fn main() { let opt: Option<i32> = None; opt.expect("should have value"); }

false_positives.name: unwrap_in_test
false_positives.code: #[cfg(test)] mod tests { #[test] fn test_unwrap() { let opt = Some(42); let val = opt.unwrap(); } }

positive.name: direct_array_index
positive.code: fn main() { let arr = [1, 2, 3]; let x = arr[5]; }

negative.name: safe_get_index
negative.code: fn main() { let arr = [1, 2, 3]; if let Some(x) = arr.get(5) { println!("{}", x); } }

negative.name: iterator_loop
negative.code: fn main() { let arr = [1, 2, 3]; for x in &arr { println!("{}", x); } }

edge_cases.name: variable_index
edge_cases.code: fn main() { let arr = [1, 2, 3]; let i = 5; let x = arr[i]; }

false_positives.name: bounds_checked_index
false_positives.code: fn main() { let arr = [1, 2, 3]; let i = 5; if i < arr.len() { let x = arr[i]; } }

positive.name: code_after_return
positive.code: fn foo() { return; println!("dead"); }

positive.name: code_after_panic
positive.code: fn foo() { panic!("error"); println!("dead"); }

negative.name: valid_code
negative.code: fn foo() { println!("alive"); }

negative.name: exhaustive_match
negative.code: fn foo(opt: Option<i32>) -> i32 { match opt { Some(x) => x, None => 0, } }

edge_cases.name: unreachable_panic_arm
edge_cases.code: fn foo(x: i32) -> i32 { match x { 1 => 10, _ => panic!("unreachable"), } }
edge_cases.reason: Panic in match is intentional exhaustive handling

false_positives.name: dead_code_in_test
false_positives.code: #[cfg(test)] mod tests { #[test] fn test_dead() { return; println!("dead but in test"); } }

positive.name: nan_equality
positive.code: fn main() { let x: f32 = 0.0/0.0; if x == x { println!("nan"); } }

positive.name: f32_inequality
positive.code: fn main() { let x: f32 = 0.0/0.0; if x != x { println!("nan"); } }

negative.name: ordering_comparison
negative.code: fn main() { let x: f32 = 1.0; if x > 0.0 { println!("positive"); } }

negative.name: nan_check_first
negative.code: fn main() { let x: f32 = 0.0/0.0; if !x.is_nan() && x > 0.0 { println!("ok"); } }

edge_cases.name: f64_nan
edge_cases.code: fn main() { let x: f64 = 0.0/0.0; if x == x { } }

positive.name: addition_overflow
positive.code: fn main() { let x: i32 = 2147483647; let y = x + 1; }

positive.name: multiplication_overflow
positive.code: fn main() { let x: i32 = 1000000; let y = x * x; }

negative.name: checked_add
negative.code: fn main() { let x: i32 = 2147483647; let y = x.checked_add(1); }

negative.name: saturating_mul
negative.code: fn main() { let x: i32 = 1000000; let y = x.saturating_mul(x); }

edge_cases.name: unsigned_overflow
edge_cases.code: fn main() { let x: u8 = 255; let y = x + 1; }

false_positives.name: wrapping_type
false_positives.code: fn main() { let x: std::num::Wrapping<i32> = Wrapping(2147483647); let y = x + Wrapping(1); }

positive.name: unwrap_none
positive.code: fn main() { let opt: Option<i32> = None; opt.unwrap(); }

positive.name: expect_none
positive.code: fn main() { let opt: Option<i32> = None; opt.expect("msg"); }

negative.name: unwrap_or
negative.code: fn main() { let opt: Option<i32> = None; let val = opt.unwrap_or(0); }

negative.name: question_mark
negative.code: fn main() -> Result<i32, ()> { let opt: Option<i32> = Some(42); Ok(opt?) }

edge_cases.name: unwrap_result_err
edge_cases.code: fn main() { let res: Result<i32, &str> = Err("error"); res.unwrap(); }

positive.name: regex_new
positive.code: use regex::Regex; fn main() { let _ = Regex::new("["); }

negative.name: lazy_regex
negative.code: use lazy_regex::lazy_regex; fn main() { let _ = lazy_regex!("[a-z]+"); }

negative.name: regex_macro
negative.code: use regex::regex; fn main() { let _ = regex!("[a-z]+"); }

edge_cases.name: regex_builder
edge_cases.code: use regex::RegexBuilder; fn main() { let _ = RegexBuilder::new("[").build(); }

false_positives.name: validated_regex
false_positives.code: use regex::Regex; fn main() { if let Ok(re) = Regex::new("[") { } }

positive.name: inclusive_range_with_len
positive.code: fn main() { let arr = vec![1,2,3]; for i in 0..=arr.len() { let _ = arr.get(i); } }

negative.name: exclusive_range
negative.code: fn main() { let arr = vec![1,2,3]; for i in 0..arr.len() { let _ = arr.get(i); } }

negative.name: iterator_loop
negative.code: fn main() { let arr = vec![1,2,3]; for x in &arr { println!("{}", x); } }

edge_cases.name: range_to_len
edge_cases.code: fn main() { let arr = vec![1,2,3]; for i in 0..=arr.len()-1 { let _ = arr[i]; } }

false_positives.name: with_bounds_check
false_positives.code: fn main() { let arr = vec![1,2,3]; for i in 0..=arr.len() { if i < arr.len() { let _ = arr[i]; } } }

positive.name: explicit_drop
positive.code: fn main() { let x = String::from("hello"); drop(x); }

negative.name: mem_forget
negative.code: use std::mem; fn main() { let x = String::from("hello"); mem::forget(x); }

negative.name: different_variables
negative.code: fn main() { let x = String::from("hello"); let y = x; drop(y); }

false_positives.name: after_move
false_positives.code: fn main() { let x = String::from("hello"); let y = x; drop(y); }
false_positives.reason: x was moved to y, not dropped twice

positive.name: mixed_operators
positive.code: fn main() { if a || b && c { } }

negative.name: parenthesized
negative.code: fn main() { if (a || b) && c { } }

negative.name: only_and
negative.code: fn main() { if a && b && c { } }

positive.name: obvious_contradiction
positive.code: fn main() { let x = 5; if x > 10 && x < 5 { } }

negative.name: valid_range
negative.code: fn main() { let x = 5; if x > 0 && x < 10 { } }

negative.name: different_vars
negative.code: fn main() { let x = 5; let y = 10; if x > 0 && y < 10 { } }

edge_cases.name: same_value_equality
edge_cases.code: fn main() { let x = 5; if x >= 5 && x < 5 { } }

false_positives.name: float_comparison
false_positives.reason: Float comparisons can have special cases

positive.name: immediate_shadow
positive.code: fn main() { let x = 5; let x = 10; println!("{}", x); }

negative.name: loop_accumulator
negative.code: fn main() { let mut x = 0; for i in 0..10 { x += i; } }

negative.name: swap_pattern
negative.code: fn main() { let mut a = 1; let mut b = 2; let temp = a; a = b; b = temp; }

edge_cases.name: debug_use
edge_cases.code: fn main() { let x = 5; println!("{}", x); let x = 10; }
edge_cases.reason: First x is used in debug print

false_positives.name: conditional_assignment
false_positives.code: fn main() { let x; if cond { x = 5; } else { x = 10; } println!("{}", x); }
